TOP NEWS " Arrest warrants issued for Texas House Democrats breaking quorum to block GOP redistricting vote," Austin American-Statesman'sJohn C. Moritz \-- "Republican House Speaker Dustin Burrows issued civil arrest warrants Monday for the more than 50 Democrats who fled Texas to block a vote on the controversial plan to boost the GOP's numbers in Congress by redistricting. "They've left the state ... and turned their backs on the constituents they swore to represent," Burrows told the mostly Republican** House members who did attend the floor session. "They've shirked their responsibilities under the direction of pressure of out of state politicians and activists who don't know the first thing about what's right for Texas." Shortly after, Gov.Greg Abbott ordered Texas Department of Public Safety officers "to locate, arrest, and return to the House chamber any member who has abandoned their duty to Texans." The warrants allow law enforcement officers to compel the return of absent members found to be in Texas, but they are not subject to criminal arrest for breaking quorum. They don't authorize officers to go out of state to bring back the dozens of Democrats who decamped to Illinois, New York and Massachusetts to deny the House the quorum it needs to conduct business. Just six of the House's 62 Democratic members were present for the seven-minute floor session. Burrows, R-Lubbock, told the chamber, and repeated during a brief news conference later, that the House will pass the redistricting plan sooner or later. The new congressional map proposed by Texas Republicans would add five more winnable GOP seats by wiping out a Democratic-leaning district in Dallas, Austin and Houston, and adding additional Republican voters to two districts in South Texas currently held by Democrats Henry Cuellar andVicente Gonzalez. "Leaving the state does not stop this House from doing its work, it only delays it," the first-term speaker said. "And every day this chamber is unable to act, the cost grows." The "cost" likely refers to the $500 per day fine that comes with breaking quorum, and which was triggered with the warrants Burrows signed. Under House rules, absent members are required to pay the fines with their own money, which means funds raised for their political accounts may not be tapped. While Democrats cast their decision to leave the state halfway through the special legislative session called by Abbott as a principled move to protect mostly voters of color from being denied representation in Congress, Burrows noted that the fate of other matters, particularly relief for the Central Texas residents whose lives were upended by last month's deadly flooding, was being jeopardized by their actions. "You understand that the issue before us, disaster recovery, fighting for the families who lost loved ones in the floods, human trafficking and more, are not abstract policy debates," Burrows said as if he were speaking to the absent Democrats. "They are real world problems demanding immediate solutions." Democratic state Rep. Terry Canales of Edinburg said he stayed in Texas both as an acknowledgement the outcome of redistricting is inevitable and "to have a seat at the table" in case Republicans were in the mood to compromise. The other Democrats who stayed in Texas were Richard Raymond of Laredo, Amando Martinez of Weslaco, Joe Moody of El Paso, Oscar Longoria of Mission, andSergio Munoz also of Mission. "Nobody could explain to me what the end game was," Canales said in a short interview. "I know how this ends. I can count to 76." Even if House Democrats block a quorum for until the special session ends on Aug. 19, nothing is stopping Abbott from calling another. When House Democrats broke quorum in 2021, then-SpeakerDade Phelan also issued warrants to bring them back. But Democrats finally returned to Austin voluntarily." * **TOP NEWS**

**" Arrest warrants issued for Texas House Democrats breaking quorum to block GOP redistricting vote," **Austin American-Statesman's**John C. Moritz** \-- "Republican House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** issued civil arrest warrants Monday for the more than 50 Democrats who fled Texas to block a vote on the controversial plan to boost the GOP's numbers in Congress by redistricting.

"They've left the state ... and turned their backs on the constituents they swore to represent," Burrows told the mostly Republican**** House members who did attend the floor session. "They've shirked their responsibilities under the direction of pressure of out of state politicians and activists who don't know the first thing about what's right for Texas."
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/ea9ef00f-0424-4d70-85cf-f11c1eb9b55f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Shortly after, Gov.**Greg Abbott** ordered Texas Department of Public Safety officers "to locate, arrest, and return to the House chamber any member who has abandoned their duty to Texans."

The warrants allow law enforcement officers to compel the return of absent members found to be in Texas, but they are not subject to criminal arrest for breaking quorum. They don't authorize officers to go out of state to bring back the dozens of Democrats who decamped to Illinois, New York and Massachusetts to deny the House the quorum it needs to conduct business.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/ea9ef00f-0424-4d70-85cf-f11c1eb9b55f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Just six of the House's 62 Democratic members were present for the seven-minute floor session.

Burrows, R-Lubbock, told the chamber, and repeated during a brief news conference later, that the House will pass the redistricting plan sooner or later.

The new congressional map proposed by Texas Republicans would add five more winnable GOP seats by wiping out a Democratic-leaning district in Dallas, Austin and Houston, and adding additional Republican voters to two districts in South Texas currently held by Democrats **Henry Cuellar** and**Vicente Gonzalez**.

"Leaving the state does not stop this House from doing its work, it only delays it," the first-term speaker said. "And every day this chamber is unable to act, the cost grows."

The "cost" likely refers to the $500 per day fine that comes with breaking quorum, and which was triggered with the warrants Burrows signed. Under House rules, absent members are required to pay the fines with their own money, which means funds raised for their political accounts may not be tapped.

While Democrats cast their decision to leave the state halfway through the special legislative session called by Abbott as a principled move to protect mostly voters of color from being denied representation in Congress, Burrows noted that the fate of other matters, particularly relief for the Central Texas residents whose lives were upended by last month's deadly flooding, was being jeopardized by their actions.

"You understand that the issue before us, disaster recovery, fighting for the families who lost loved ones in the floods, human trafficking and more, are not abstract policy debates," Burrows said as if he were speaking to the absent Democrats. "They are real world problems demanding immediate solutions."

Democratic state Rep. **Terry Canales** of Edinburg said he stayed in Texas both as an acknowledgement the outcome of redistricting is inevitable and "to have a seat at the table" in case Republicans were in the mood to compromise.

The other Democrats who stayed in Texas were **Richard Raymond** of Laredo, **Amando Martinez** of Weslaco, **Joe Moody** of El Paso, **Oscar Longoria** of Mission, and**Sergio Munoz** also of Mission.

"Nobody could explain to me what the end game was," Canales said in a short interview. "I know how this ends. I can count to 76."

Even if House Democrats block a quorum for until the special session ends on Aug. 19, nothing is stopping Abbott from calling another.

When House Democrats broke quorum in 2021, then-Speaker**Dade Phelan** also issued warrants to bring them back. But Democrats finally returned to Austin voluntarily." TOP NEWS " Arrest warrants issued for Texas House Democrats breaking quorum to block GOP redistricting vote," Austin American-Statesman'sJohn C. Moritz \-- "Republican House Speaker Dustin Burrows issued civil arrest warrants Monday for the more than 50 Democrats who fled Texas to block a vote on the controversial plan to boost the GOP's numbers in Congress by redistricting. "They've left the state ... and turned their backs on the constituents they swore to represent," Burrows told the mostly Republican** House members who did attend the floor session. "They've shirked their responsibilities under the direction of pressure of out of state politicians and activists who don't know the first thing about what's right for Texas." Shortly after, Gov.Greg Abbott ordered Texas Department of Public Safety officers "to locate, arrest, and return to the House chamber any member who has abandoned their duty to Texans." The warrants allow law enforcement officers to compel the return of absent members found to be in Texas, but they are not subject to criminal arrest for breaking quorum. They don't authorize officers to go out of state to bring back the dozens of Democrats who decamped to Illinois, New York and Massachusetts to deny the House the quorum it needs to conduct business. Just six of the House's 62 Democratic members were present for the seven-minute floor session. Burrows, R-Lubbock, told the chamber, and repeated during a brief news conference later, that the House will pass the redistricting plan sooner or later. The new congressional map proposed by Texas Republicans would add five more winnable GOP seats by wiping out a Democratic-leaning district in Dallas, Austin and Houston, and adding additional Republican voters to two districts in South Texas currently held by Democrats Henry Cuellar andVicente Gonzalez. "Leaving the state does not stop this House from doing its work, it only delays it," the first-term speaker said. "And every day this chamber is unable to act, the cost grows." The "cost" likely refers to the $500 per day fine that comes with breaking quorum, and which was triggered with the warrants Burrows signed. Under House rules, absent members are required to pay the fines with their own money, which means funds raised for their political accounts may not be tapped. While Democrats cast their decision to leave the state halfway through the special legislative session called by Abbott as a principled move to protect mostly voters of color from being denied representation in Congress, Burrows noted that the fate of other matters, particularly relief for the Central Texas residents whose lives were upended by last month's deadly flooding, was being jeopardized by their actions. "You understand that the issue before us, disaster recovery, fighting for the families who lost loved ones in the floods, human trafficking and more, are not abstract policy debates," Burrows said as if he were speaking to the absent Democrats. "They are real world problems demanding immediate solutions." Democratic state Rep. Terry Canales of Edinburg said he stayed in Texas both as an acknowledgement the outcome of redistricting is inevitable and "to have a seat at the table" in case Republicans were in the mood to compromise. The other Democrats who stayed in Texas were Richard Raymond of Laredo, Amando Martinez of Weslaco, Joe Moody of El Paso, Oscar Longoria of Mission, andSergio Munoz also of Mission. "Nobody could explain to me what the end game was," Canales said in a short interview. "I know how this ends. I can count to 76." Even if House Democrats block a quorum for until the special session ends on Aug. 19, nothing is stopping Abbott from calling another. When House Democrats broke quorum in 2021, then-SpeakerDade Phelan also issued warrants to bring them back. But Democrats finally returned to Austin voluntarily." * **TOP NEWS**

**“Arrest warrants issued for Texas House Democrats breaking quorum to block GOP redistricting vote,”** Austin American-Statesman's**John C. Moritz** \-- "Republican House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** issued civil arrest warrants Monday for the more than 50 Democrats who fled Texas to block a vote on the controversial plan to boost the GOP's numbers in Congress by redistricting.

"They've left the state ... and turned their backs on the constituents they swore to represent," Burrows told the mostly Republican**** House members who did attend the floor session. "They've shirked their responsibilities under the direction of pressure of out of state politicians and activists who don't know the first thing about what's right for Texas."

Shortly after, Gov.**Greg Abbott** ordered Texas Department of Public Safety officers "to locate, arrest, and return to the House chamber any member who has abandoned their duty to Texans."

The warrants allow law enforcement officers to compel the return of absent members found to be in Texas, but they are not subject to criminal arrest for breaking quorum. They don't authorize officers to go out of state to bring back the dozens of Democrats who decamped to Illinois, New York and Massachusetts to deny the House the quorum it needs to conduct business.

Just six of the House's 62 Democratic members were present for the seven-minute floor session.

Burrows, R-Lubbock, told the chamber, and repeated during a brief news conference later, that the House will pass the redistricting plan sooner or later.

The new congressional map proposed by Texas Republicans would add five more winnable GOP seats by wiping out a Democratic-leaning district in Dallas, Austin and Houston, and adding additional Republican voters to two districts in South Texas currently held by Democrats **Henry Cuellar** and**Vicente Gonzalez**.

"Leaving the state does not stop this House from doing its work, it only delays it," the first-term speaker said. "And every day this chamber is unable to act, the cost grows."

The "cost" likely refers to the $500 per day fine that comes with breaking quorum, and which was triggered with the warrants Burrows signed. Under House rules, absent members are required to pay the fines with their own money, which means funds raised for their political accounts may not be tapped.

While Democrats cast their decision to leave the state halfway through the special legislative session called by Abbott as a principled move to protect mostly voters of color from being denied representation in Congress, Burrows noted that the fate of other matters, particularly relief for the Central Texas residents whose lives were upended by last month's deadly flooding, was being jeopardized by their actions.

"You understand that the issue before us, disaster recovery, fighting for the families who lost loved ones in the floods, human trafficking and more, are not abstract policy debates," Burrows said as if he were speaking to the absent Democrats. "They are real world problems demanding immediate solutions."

Democratic state Rep. **Terry Canales** of Edinburg said he stayed in Texas both as an acknowledgement the outcome of redistricting is inevitable and "to have a seat at the table" in case Republicans were in the mood to compromise.

The other Democrats who stayed in Texas were **Richard Raymond** of Laredo, **Amando Martinez** of Weslaco, **Joe Moody** of El Paso, **Oscar Longoria** of Mission, and**Sergio Munoz** also of Mission.

"Nobody could explain to me what the end game was," Canales said in a short interview. "I know how this ends. I can count to 76."

Even if House Democrats block a quorum for until the special session ends on Aug. 19, nothing is stopping Abbott from calling another.

When House Democrats broke quorum in 2021, then-Speaker**Dade Phelan** also issued warrants to bring them back. But Democrats finally returned to Austin voluntarily." Our concise, 5-minute read lands in your inbox each morning at no cost. Experience news without the noise; let 1440 help you make up your own mind. Sign up now and invite your friends and family to be part of the informed. | TOP NEWS “Arrest warrants issued for Texas House Democrats breaking quorum to block GOP redistricting vote,” Austin American-Statesman'sJohn C. Moritz \-- "Republican House Speaker Dustin Burrows issued civil arrest warrants Monday for the more than 50 Democrats who fled Texas to block a vote on the controversial plan to boost the GOP's numbers in Congress by redistricting. "They've left the state ... and turned their backs on the constituents they swore to represent," Burrows told the mostly Republican** House members who did attend the floor session. "They've shirked their responsibilities under the direction of pressure of out of state politicians and activists who don't know the first thing about what's right for Texas." » Shortly after, Gov.Greg Abbott ordered Texas Department of Public Safety officers "to locate, arrest, and return to the House chamber any member who has abandoned their duty to Texans." The warrants allow law enforcement officers to compel the return of absent members found to be in Texas, but they are not subject to criminal arrest for breaking quorum. They don't authorize officers to go out of state to bring back the dozens of Democrats who decamped to Illinois, New York and Massachusetts to deny the House the quorum it needs to conduct business. » Just six of the House's 62 Democratic members were present for the seven-minute floor session. Burrows, R-Lubbock, told the chamber, and repeated during a brief news conference later, that the House will pass the redistricting plan sooner or later. The new congressional map proposed by Texas Republicans would add five more winnable GOP seats by wiping out a Democratic-leaning district in Dallas, Austin and Houston, and adding additional Republican voters to two districts in South Texas currently held by Democrats Henry Cuellar andVicente Gonzalez. "Leaving the state does not stop this House from doing its work, it only delays it," the first-term speaker said. "And every day this chamber is unable to act, the cost grows." The "cost" likely refers to the $500 per day fine that comes with breaking quorum, and which was triggered with the warrants Burrows signed. Under House rules, absent members are required to pay the fines with their own money, which means funds raised for their political accounts may not be tapped. While Democrats cast their decision to leave the state halfway through the special legislative session called by Abbott as a principled move to protect mostly voters of color from being denied representation in Congress, Burrows noted that the fate of other matters, particularly relief for the Central Texas residents whose lives were upended by last month's deadly flooding, was being jeopardized by their actions. "You understand that the issue before us, disaster recovery, fighting for the families who lost loved ones in the floods, human trafficking and more, are not abstract policy debates," Burrows said as if he were speaking to the absent Democrats. "They are real world problems demanding immediate solutions." Democratic state Rep. Terry Canales of Edinburg said he stayed in Texas both as an acknowledgement the outcome of redistricting is inevitable and "to have a seat at the table" in case Republicans were in the mood to compromise. The other Democrats who stayed in Texas were Richard Raymond of Laredo, Amando Martinez of Weslaco, Joe Moody of El Paso, Oscar Longoria of Mission, andSergio Munoz also of Mission. "Nobody could explain to me what the end game was," Canales said in a short interview. "I know how this ends. I can count to 76." Even if House Democrats block a quorum for until the special session ends on Aug. 19, nothing is stopping Abbott from calling another. When House Democrats broke quorum in 2021, then-SpeakerDade Phelan also issued warrants to bring them back. But Democrats finally returned to Austin voluntarily." GOP Rep. Harriet Hageman **[stood her ground against Jerry Nadler](https://substack.com/redirect/e3dd849c-cb74-4a95-856e-b442a20ee80c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** after he targeted her with an absurd tirade, calling her bill "evil" and lashing out about transgender women's rights to compete against biological women in athletic competition.
"Every time that a man competes against a woman, they're probably going to beat them, so I don't think we need that report!"

name: Janak Joshi for U.S Senate
subject: This Senate race will send SHOCKWAVES through Washington.
body:
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]

Fellow patriot,

I didn’t break the law to come to the United States.

I followed the rules. I worked hard to EARN my citizenship.

Now, I’m a PROUD AMERICAN, so why does the Radical Left want my
head?
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]

Frankly, the people we’re up against would rather give YOUR tax
dollars to Tren de Arugua CRIMINALS than admit our country is BACK on
the right track.

_LET ME INTRODUCE MYSELF. MY NAME IS DR. JANAK JOSHI, AND TODAY, I’M
OFFICIALLY LAUNCHING MY CAMPAIGN FOR THE UNITED STATES SENATE IN
COLORADO._

I’m not a career politician. I’m a physician, a patriot, and a
proud immigrant who came to America with nothing more than a suitcase,
$100, and a dream.
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]

I worked day and night to make that dream real.

For 30 years, I served Colorado Springs families as a doctor. I opened
a medical clinic and dialysis centers, providing care to my neighbors
and employing dozens of hardworking Coloradans.

I gave back to the community I love—serving on the Crime Stoppers
Board, volunteering at local clinics, and supporting nonprofits that
keep our neighborhoods safe.

Colorado allowed me to achieve the American Dream. And now, I’m
running for Senate to protect that dream.

But here’s the truth:
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]

The Radical Left is tearing that dream apart.

They’ve opened our borders, embraced lawlessness, and treated
law-abiding Americans and legal immigrants like me as second-class
citizens.

My opponent? Votes lockstep with AOC and Schumer:

Soft on crime.

Pro-illegal immigration.

Silent on sanctuary city chaos.

Enough is enough.
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]

I REFUSE to stand by while career politicians sell out the people of
Colorado.

That’s why I’m running for U.S. Senate
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]to
SECURE our border, RESTORE the rule of law, END wasteful spending, and
put Colorado back at the heart of America’s economic engine.

This campaign isn’t about me. It’s about you, friend, and every
American who refuses to watch the Radical Left AID and ABET the
invasion and destruction of our country.

I didn’t come to this country and earn my citizenship just to see it
turned into a third-world hellhole.

BUT TO STOP THAT, I’LL NEED YOU

The Democrat machine knows I’m a THREAT.

They’re dumping millions into Colorado to SMEAR MY NAME and protect
this vulnerable Senate seat.

If patriots like you stand with me from day one, we can flip this seat
red and deliver a MAJOR America First victory in 2026.

WILL YOU BE ONE OF MY FIRST 500 FOUNDING DONORS TO THIS HISTORIC
CAMPAIGN?
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]

Your support today is how we show the Radical Left that the silent
majority is ready to ROAR.

>>> BECOME A FOUNDING DONOR <<<
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]

Thank you for believing in me. From a young man with $100 in his
pocket to a U.S. Senate candidate, it’s only possible because of
America and supporters like you.

LET’S TAKE OUR COUNTRY BACK—TOGETHER.

God bless you,

DR. JANAK JOSHI

_Republican for U.S. Senate – Colorado_

P.S. This is our chance to send a message: Law and order still matter.
Stand with me today and help launch this campaign with unstoppable
momentum!

[ CHIP IN HERE ]
[https://secure.winred.com/janak-joshi-for-colorado/joshi_flex8634_luanch1_eml_w_20250804?utm_campaign=067000000083020&utm_medium=email&xuno=464c455838363334&recurring=true&utm_term=a-m]

PAID AND AUTHORIZED BY JANAK JOSHI FOR SENATE

[http://%7B%7BunsubscribeUrl%7D%7D]

Unsubscribe: https://app.vbout.com/index.php?r=Email/OptoutConfirmationForm&messageid=308768&trkid=828113999&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com

215 N Payne Street, Alexandria, VA 22314

name: Teichert in the Trenches
subject: Monthly Newsletter - August 2025
body:
I continue to be honored and humbled to be...
---
| | | | | |
---
| |
#### Teichert in the Trenches - August 2025
Welcome to this month’s installment of Teichert in the Trenches, a monthly newsletter highlighting a snippet of my attempts to maximize my impact on people and our great nation and to develop the leaders our nation needs! ** _Teichert in the News_**
---
| | | | [ ](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeFuBmP64SPACXkt7Xr7g/AH5zZCkL5C_w)
---
| | | The topics of Ukraine/Russia and Israel/Hamas dominated the international affairs news in July. I appreciated the opportunity to chat about both with Molly Line on Fox News. (July 12)
---
| [ Addressing the Bottleneck of Business in Prince George's](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeG13EXv1dZJgdaBrW9hw/8A3exAaCLWF7)
---
🦀 Striving to Wrestle Our Community Back to Where it Belongs! 📣 I continue to be honored and humbled to be the Chair of the Community Advisory Council in Prince George's County, Maryland, serving under the Business Roundtable. We are charged with making recommendations to improve Educational Outcomes, Economic Opportunities, and Public Safety Underpinnings. 🔥 I deeply appreciated the willingness of the community to hear some hard truths at this session on economic barriers that create an anti-growth instead of a pro-growth climate. I am thankful to serve alongside a great team of community leaders and subject matter experts! ** _Thoughts By Teichert_**
| | | |
---
| | | | [ The Dragon Who Earned His Wings](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeG7uggjyojTAjPUbUSIC/XTdWcV3ZhH98 "US absence in the world is as dangerous as US weakness")
---
🐲 The Dragon Who Earned His Wings is flying off the Amazon shelves (pun intended). 📣 There are a few of items from the book that the casual reader wouldn’t know: ✈️ General Chuck Yeager was a major inspiration for the entire book!
1️⃣ Like John the Dragon, he also got sick on his first few flights
2️⃣ There is a casual reference to “the right stuff” towards the end of the book
3️⃣ The book was released on July 1st, which is the month when enlisted aircraft maintainer Yeager was accepted into a pilot training. 🧡 John the Dragon is always wearing an orange lanyard, a shout out to the flight test community that embraces the color orange.
| | | | [ "Setting Conditions for a Middle East that can Benefit the US"](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGEm8pYvztcepEnLSksS/FpOONI4GhWql "Prayers for a Nation")
---
| | | Thanks to Real Clear Defense for publishing my thoughts on the revolutionary situation in the Middle East. “We are witnessing meaningful change in the Middle East for the first time in decades. Americans should feel a sense of hope that the region can offer benefits to the United States rather than simply draining its resources ... For the first time in my adult life, Americans can envision a Middle East that benefits the United States instead of simply bankrupting us.”
---
| | | | [ "Inside America’s Response to Middle East Instability"](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGLdayNtB3m8v465R3Si/NjjcUSnMK3uU)
---
Thanks to Jenn Nassour for the invite to join her on the “Political Contessa” podcast! We covered a wide range of topics including the Middle East, leadership, communication, and politics.
| [ “Success Isn’t Promotion”](https://eufi5.r.a.d.sendibm1.com/mk/cl/f/sh/SMK1E8tHeGSV37CqMDvd0tOpPM2y/4aewbfBQUL_I)
---
| | | I thoroughly enjoyed joining Thomas Helfrich on his “Cut the Tie” podcast. We talked about how to move to the next level as a leader, and I appreciated the unexpected opportunity to share about the overlap between secular and spiritual leadership principles.
---
|
---
📣 A Pastor to Pastors – the power of intentionally developing leaders! ⛪ Pastor and preacher John MacArthur passed away this past month. Not only was he diligent about performing his role in his own church in service to the Lord, but he was deliberate and intentional about investing himself in the development and encouragement of other spiritual leaders. All leaders should prioritize such activity! !!️ I was honored to host MacArthur for a tour of the F-22 Combined Test Force during my time as squadron commander. While our time together on that day was brief, it was his impact on my pastors that made an indirect lasting difference in my life!
|
---
⚾ A Streak and a Leadership Lesson for the Ages!

🔥 On July 17th, 1941, Joe DiMaggio’s 56-game hitting streak ended. It is a record that remains today, with no one ever coming close.

📣 DiMaggio played every inning of every game with energy and gusto. When asked why, he There is always some kid who may be seeing me for the first or last time. I owe him my best!”

💥 What a great leadership lesson! First impressions, last impressions, and everything in between matters! Leaders are always on! We must show up and consistently deliver! People – on our team and from the outside – are constantly watching! They are carefully judging what they think about us and our team!

!!️ We owe them our best! ** _Teichert in the Trenches_**
| | |
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| | | |
---
| | | **_Behind the Scenes_** Here are a few behind-the-scenes elements of my TV interviews that you will likely appreciate and may not know about:
---
| | |
---
| |
---
| | | Sometimes when a news station wants it to look like you are in the studio, but don’t want you to make your way into their actual studio, they send a van to work movie magic. In these situations, I walk outside and sit in the van at the base of my driveway to do the interview while it looks like I’m at the studio with the host. Here is a side-by-side picture of the live show and the outside of the van while I’m filming.
---
| | | |
---
When they actually want you in studio, they typically provide transportation.
| | |
---
| |
---
| | | And as you wait to be taken down the hall to the studio for an interview, you spend time in the green room. I especially like the painting of Charles Krauthammer in the Fox News green room in DC – a giant of a man and media professional. ** _My Bookshelf_**
---
|
---
“Fellow patriots, the power of one is amazing, but when we gather together and harness the power of thousands and millions of likeminded people, we become a force that can’t be stopped. What can you do? Pray! Share your faith. Volunteer during the election season to help get the vote out for candidates who share your values. Research the candidates. Get others involved. Speak up for what you believe. And be willing to take a stand even if nobody else will. God will honor that.”
|
---
“How did we get to this state of division? We could blame the radicalization of our universities, the degradation of our culture, or the politicization of our everyday lives. We could blame America’s disunity on the breakdown of the family, the incivility we see in the news and on social media, or the church’s loss of cultural influence. But it all comes down to a simple answer: We have forgotten God. When people no longer love God, they can no longer love themselves rightly. The equation is ** _PLUS Posts for the Month_**

clean_body: I continue to be honored and humbled to be... | | | | | | | | Teichert in the Trenches - August 2025 Welcome to this month’s installment of Teichert in the Trenches, a monthly newsletter highlighting a snippet of my attempts to maximize my impact on people and our great nation and to develop the leaders our nation needs! Teichert in the News | | | | | | | The topics of Ukraine/Russia and Israel/Hamas dominated the international affairs news in July. I appreciated the opportunity to chat about both with Molly Line on Fox News. (July 12) | 🦀 Striving to Wrestle Our Community Back to Where it Belongs! 📣 I continue to be honored and humbled to be the Chair of the Community Advisory Council in Prince George's County, Maryland, serving under the Business Roundtable. We are charged with making recommendations to improve Educational Outcomes, Economic Opportunities, and Public Safety Underpinnings. 🔥 I deeply appreciated the willingness of the community to hear some hard truths at this session on economic barriers that create an anti-growth instead of a pro-growth climate. I am thankful to serve alongside a great team of community leaders and subject matter experts! Thoughts By Teichert | | | | | | | | "US absence in the world is as dangerous as US weakness" 🐲 The Dragon Who Earned His Wings is flying off the Amazon shelves (pun intended). 📣 There are a few of items from the book that the casual reader wouldn’t know: ✈️ General Chuck Yeager was a major inspiration for the entire book! 1️⃣ Like John the Dragon, he also got sick on his first few flights 2️⃣ There is a casual reference to “the right stuff” towards the end of the book 3️⃣ The book was released on July 1st, which is the month when enlisted aircraft maintainer Yeager was accepted into a pilot training. 🧡 John the Dragon is always wearing an orange lanyard, a shout out to the flight test community that embraces the color orange. | | | | "Prayers for a Nation" | | | Thanks to Real Clear Defense for publishing my thoughts on the revolutionary situation in the Middle East. “We are witnessing meaningful change in the Middle East for the first time in decades. Americans should feel a sense of hope that the region can offer benefits to the United States rather than simply draining its resources ... For the first time in my adult life, Americans can envision a Middle East that benefits the United States instead of simply bankrupting us.” | | | | Thanks to Jenn Nassour for the invite to join her on the “Political Contessa” podcast! We covered a wide range of topics including the Middle East, leadership, communication, and politics. | | | | I thoroughly enjoyed joining Thomas Helfrich on his “Cut the Tie” podcast. We talked about how to move to the next level as a leader, and I appreciated the unexpected opportunity to share about the overlap between secular and spiritual leadership principles. | 📣 A Pastor to Pastors – the power of intentionally developing leaders! ⛪ Pastor and preacher John MacArthur passed away this past month. Not only was he diligent about performing his role in his own church in service to the Lord, but he was deliberate and intentional about investing himself in the development and encouragement of other spiritual leaders. All leaders should prioritize such activity! !!️ I was honored to host MacArthur for a tour of the F-22 Combined Test Force during my time as squadron commander. While our time together on that day was brief, it was his impact on my pastors that made an indirect lasting difference in my life! | ⚾ A Streak and a Leadership Lesson for the Ages! 🔥 On July 17th, 1941, Joe DiMaggio’s 56-game hitting streak ended. It is a record that remains today, with no one ever coming close. 📣 DiMaggio played every inning of every game with energy and gusto. When asked why, he There is always some kid who may be seeing me for the first or last time. I owe him my best!” 💥 What a great leadership lesson! First impressions, last impressions, and everything in between matters! Leaders are always on! We must show up and consistently deliver! People – on our team and from the outside – are constantly watching! They are carefully judging what they think about us and our team! !!️ We owe them our best! Teichert in the Trenches | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Behind the Scenes Here are a few behind-the-scenes elements of my TV interviews that you will likely appreciate and may not know about: | | | | | | | | Sometimes when a news station wants it to look like you are in the studio, but don’t want you to make your way into their actual studio, they send a van to work movie magic. In these situations, I walk outside and sit in the van at the base of my driveway to do the interview while it looks like I’m at the studio with the host. Here is a side-by-side picture of the live show and the outside of the van while I’m filming. | | | | When they actually want you in studio, they typically provide transportation. | | | | | | | | And as you wait to be taken down the hall to the studio for an interview, you spend time in the green room. I especially like the painting of Charles Krauthammer in the Fox News green room in DC – a giant of a man and media professional. My Bookshelf | “Fellow patriots, the power of one is amazing, but when we gather together and harness the power of thousands and millions of likeminded people, we become a force that can’t be stopped. What can you do? Pray! Share your faith. Volunteer during the election season to help get the vote out for candidates who share your values. Research the candidates. Get others involved. Speak up for what you believe. And be willing to take a stand even if nobody else will. God will honor that.” | “How did we get to this state of division? The title of the article says it all: “Americans Reject Isolationism, Embrace American Leadership and Economic Engagement.” A huge thanks to Roger Zakheim and his incredible team for revealing an important truth about the stalwart American Spirit! name: Ted Cruz
subject: I need to ask you something that might feel small
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
But it matters a lot

Fellow Conservative,

**[I need to ask you something that might feel small-- but it matters a lot.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z1e2queqbjic6sj7s188do8gc7cmjbl5ig7plk8o_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i62p30cjh2bs)**

**[Will you _chip in just $1_ to help us stop the Democrats from taking the Senate?](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zu0hv5vd7urt5ck1suf0h97192nb3q94ke7aubco_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i62p30cjh2bs)**

You may not have donated in a while. I get it. But if you ever believed in what we've done together -- the tax cuts, the conservative judges, the border wall -- **[I'm asking you to come off the sidelines for just one dollar.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2znk9o6mq2luanff1lsintpj5s2m73h9t5i8lv0m0_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i62p30cjh2bs)**

Because just a couple weeks ago, while you were going about your life, the Senate passed President Trump's "Big Beautiful Bill" and it was signed into law just days later. We pulled an all-nighter to get it done. Why?

**[Because it locks in permanent tax relief, imposes work requirements for welfare, and invests over $170 BILLION into border security, including:](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z04mjfbp3rrrmsra0grp2o34brq2u3ts3pjnqle0_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i62p30cjh2bs)**
1. $46.5 billion for the wall
2. $45 billion to expand detention facilities
3. $29.9 billion to hire 10,000 new ICE agents

This is real progress -- the kind we dreamed about just a few years ago. But now the Democrats smell blood. They're outraising us, outspending us, and targeting our Senate seats -- even in red states

**[They want revenge.](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zn5is1d9gda48m4ifiqcg05doupfg1h4k23l3a1o_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i62p30cjh2bs)**

If we lose this next election, everything we just won could vanish. They'll tear it all down.

That's why I'm writing you -- not for $10, not for $100 -- just for $1. Not because $1 will flip the Senate, but because it's a signal that you haven't given up.
[CHIP IN $1 NOW](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z60kdedr1ijtn4i0gjo1jua246qqvj1fnpvgn14g_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i62p30cjh2bs)
Your name on our donor list tells me we're not alone -- that this still matters to you.
Thank you,

[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zirf5bfmom3cdicg5bllnkkhavq1cammefp56qeo_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i62p30cjh2bs)
Ted Cruz

[CHIP IN $1 NOW](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zpo9dffh3dg4gitsfb9nj38hiaoe02mcaapdc9g0_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i62p30cjh2bs)

This is your chance to take a stand, loud and clear. | | | | | We need your response in the next 30 minutes before this list is LOCKED IN. Don’t let the swamp speak for you. Thank you, D.O.G.E. body:
Protecting Virginia's workforce is not just a "real issue," it's the job of the next Governor of Virginia.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112764869/561555979/830632682?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-08-05_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMWE4OTBkOTctZmY3MS1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=KfG8o8wUQ9MJP9uPDLBBfggF_vE4erzudaH2G29cgEk=)
Winsome Earle-Sears went on CNN this past Friday, and was asked the following: "Do you support the President's purge of the federal workforce, yes or no?"
_She refused to answer._
After my opponent dodged multiple follow-ups about DOGE, she then said, _"I want to talk about real issues."_
Let me be clear:**Protecting Virginia's workforce is not just a "real issue," it's the job of the next Governor of Virginia.**
I've listened to Virginians who've been fired. Their lives have been turned upside down, and they deserve a Governor who will stand up to the chaos coming out of Trump's Washington. I will get to work on day one putting plans in place to grow our economy, strengthen our public schools, and make our lives more affordable.
[_**But first, we need to win. The President is gearing up to go all-in for Winsome Earle-Sears, and as one of my top supporters, I hope you'll have my back once again today. After the first two polls in this race showed me leading, The Cook Political Report just upgraded our chances of winning! This is officially one of Democrats’ only pickup opportunities in the Senate. But even before this change, Mitch McConnell and the GOP were already attacking me – and now, it’s about to get much, much worse. That’s why we’ve set an emergency goal to raise $50,000 before midnight tonight, and the only way we’ll hit it and be able to fight back is if you give right now. So please, will you [make a split donation between Blue Amp Action and my campaign so we can win this seat?](https://default.salsalabs.org/T15b8d279-b3d3-4c1f-a690-8a53cf8ca093/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)

| | | About Blue Amp Action: We cannot sugarcoat the current situation. Right-wing authoritarianism, defeated by a worldwide coalition during WWII, was only in hibernation. Over the past decade it has infected democratic societies--including ours. It is as simple as it is terrifying: What we do now will define the future of our country and world. We at Blue Amp Action are dedicated to liberal, open societies, where reason and science are applied to solve our problems; where civil rights--including voting rights!--are protected for all. We fight for these values every day. Speaking up on our platforms, raising the voices and the political prospects of liberal pols and pundits. **And savvy grassroots activists like you know, just like we do, that we can’t stop watching the people we help elect:_ after the elections, we have to keep organizing support for the policies we like and pushing back against the ones we don’t._** **__When the public tunes out between elections and the only people our elected officials hear from are the paid lobbyists of wealthy corporations, our government stops being accountable to the needs of ordinary families.__** **Democracy never sleeps, and neither can we.** So we ask that you support our work, so we can continue to engage in this fight every moment of every day. So our kids grow up in democracy, not dystopia. Thank you for your support for all we do! — _You're getting this message because you’ve supported good causes and Democratic candidates. We need your support to continue fighting for our democracy. We love having you in our network, but if you no longer wish to hear from us, you can[unsubscribe here](https://default.salsalabs.org/T65550790-d8f4-43b8-8d05-19b9a4345e00/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)._

name: Dr Scott Jensen
subject: Smoke is Here. So Is Our Chance to Show Leadership
disclaimer_text: paid for by Dr. Scott Jensen for Governor
body:
| |
###### Failed leadership got us here. Fight back with Common Sense solutions.

[](http://email.mg.drscottjensen.com/c/eJxkjzuOHDEMRE-zShYa6EOKVKCDODIoiextoz-L6bZ9fWMmdVgPBVQ9-T1XPYb-XGerzOzGedwy7leOOLtBBE-Jhgdj9YJivkuW2HlKTeJms1lTNqctEgKESBzcV4vah81ScViEASOLKtc-ppChYnBrSyFh4IAx55rrQwGqAEjHSjlZ_ICwL4_5vMZ537_0uPR4jHN3W_uf7XpdsrwtiKyUgtMny9GDyPAiWD0EkqgiKSi687m8upzTLIWSt2TJA2P1HCz5UjkOwWnas3u2-f133bb1KvQBYdll3d6jdzvO4_OHfJ3n5wu6W_fvTe73DaWByFb8DNI9kEYvObGnyMhaRqRE7k9L_wIAAP__2Ft0aQ)
---
Good Afternoon, Peter

The haze in our skies isn’t just smoke, it’s a wake-up call. Once again, Minnesotans are choking on wildfire smoke. Our air quality is dangerous. Our kids, grandparents, and neighbors with asthma or heart conditions are at serious risk.

👉 **[Watch this video »
](http://email.mg.drscottjensen.com/c/eJwcj7uu2zAQRL9GbC5okMvHLgsVQQKXaYIUqYIVubR1IYmORdtIvj6QyzkzxRl-lFm2LL_nMiYiUrltnXM_sg1lqt56jYBZ-0qiOXDVEzu2ExVOwKqMtSRwVcloMXhvLJJR17EmG11EmCiLuOTYoU9AlA2aWGFS8wgGgiETrHPJpZN4n9h7nkJCB9UO3qyXU7nvufX-Kdsu2ym3VS3jtffbPrgvA5wHOL9er9Pf9uiPSY7-INzzdXDn5-C-_fnR_n3_2j5_zk2tsu98eX9FrDHGUDRUZ7Vnzpo5JO0NshVmMBJUu1-OLTkoMSLoChW0p5A0mQo6JrKZQ6kyOXUfy-01L8u8Rxy8uaw8L2_dPm5t-_jF19Y-Dqi6rLeF-1tDMIdANepieNIexWp2QBotBZKYLQKq5wj_AwAA__8fX36N)**
As a doctor, I’ve treated the damage smoke inhalation can cause. As a father and grandfather, I’m angry our families are asking: _Is it even safe to go outside?
_
And as a candidate for Governor, I’m ready to act—because this crisis demands more than empty press conferences and failed priorities.

**Governor Walz has no plan.** He’s spent billions on pet projects, ignored the warning signs, and let fraudsters run wild with our tax dollars. Meanwhile, Minnesota still has no coordinated wildfire prevention or public health emergency strategy.

Enough is enough.

**Here’s what I would do as Minnesota’s Governor:**
🔥 **Launch real wildfire prevention** —partnering with Canadian provinces, investing in better forest management, and improving early detection systems.
🩺 **Streamline medical response** —empower pharmacists to dispense emergency respiratory meds and ensure hospitals can fast-track treatment during smoke events.
🛡️ **Protect our most vulnerable** —require long-term care facilities and families to proactively secure medications and action plans for fragile patients.
🌲 **Build climate resilience** —invest in reforestation, clean energy, and smarter emission controls.

**Minnesota deserves bold, competent leadership. Let’s clear the air—and the Governor’s Office.
**
Will you step up right now to help us fight back with common sense and action?

| [DONATE NOW](http://email.mg.drscottjensen.com/c/eJwkjzuO5DoMAE9jJQM2JOpHBQ5e0nd40YKWKLcXttSwNbPXX7g3JFkFFPm7bNKy_NrKnIhI5d4G53HPxpelOuMgYszgKgmw5woLWzYLFU7Iqsy1JLRVyWyid06bSFq9ZqMxkEe9OCcGXTJoAwWOaD17a0ltM2r0mrQ31iabHuJcYud48SlarGZy-lgf5bxyH-O3tEvaI_dD7fNrjPc12f8mfE74vCR_n_LgJqWPm5jwWU74aPDPg9pPWPuPnK2f97k3HqIOuS5eP7_HWEMIvgBWa8AxZ2D2CZyObIQZtXjVz_VmyWIJISJUrAiOfALSFSEkMpl9qbJYdc7l_Wfb9-0KcXJ6PXjbP_ljbr19_c-v3r_upRpyvHcenwyJ2XuqAYrmBVwUA2yRIBryJCGbiFH9zPg3AAD__zaEhBI)
---

---
Let’s replace political smog with real solutions. Let’s win this fight—for our families, our health, and our future.

Onward,
**Dr. Scott Jensen**
Republican Candidate for Governor of Minnesota

**P.S.** The smoke is real. So is the failure in St. Paul. Let’s do something about both—donate now and help us lead.

[DONATE NOW](http://email.mg.drscottjensen.com/c/eJwkjzuO5DoMAE9jJQM2JOpHBQ5e0nd40YKWKLcXttSwNbPXX7g3JFkFFPm7bNKy_NrKnIhI5d4G53HPxpelOuMgYszgKgmw5woLWzYLFU7Iqsy1JLRVyWyid06bSFq9ZqMxkEe9OCcGXTJoAwWOaD17a0ltM2r0mrQ31iabHuJcYud48SlarGZy-lgf5bxyH-O3tEvaI_dD7fNrjPc12f8mfE74vCR_n_LgJqWPm5jwWU74aPDPg9pPWPuPnK2f97k3HqIOuS5eP7_HWEMIvgBWa8AxZ2D2CZyObIQZtXjVz_VmyWIJISJUrAiOfALSFSEkMpl9qbJYdc7l_Wfb9-0KcXJ6PXjbP_ljbr19_c-v3r_upRpyvHcenwyJ2XuqAYrmBVwUA2yRIBryJCGbiFH9zPg3AAD__zaEhBI)

name: Ted Cruz
subject: I need to ask you something that might feel small
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the NRSC
body:
But it matters a lot

Fellow Conservative,

**[I need to ask you something that might feel small-- but it matters a lot.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2ztbnko5jp3skb4hmmko51en2k0vbkqcbq0patb18_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)**

**[Will you _chip in just $1_ to help us stop the Democrats from taking the Senate?](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zvtlk0fh9t1iepdoaovfl26dfi0sv5araepokeu0_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)**

You may not have donated in a while. I get it. But if you ever believed in what we've done together -- the tax cuts, the conservative judges, the border wall -- **[I'm asking you to come off the sidelines for just one dollar.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zeesq5lphpncemr1kpmqir86pcgv47ols1quatmg_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)**

Because just a couple weeks ago, while you were going about your life, the Senate passed President Trump's "Big Beautiful Bill" and it was signed into law just days later. We pulled an all-nighter to get it done. Why?

**[Because it locks in permanent tax relief, imposes work requirements for welfare, and invests over $170 BILLION into border security, including:](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z534brioje995p9dmhhi25qheea5du5s7f2tf53o_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)**
1. $46.5 billion for the wall
2. $45 billion to expand detention facilities
3. $29.9 billion to hire 10,000 new ICE agents

This is real progress -- the kind we dreamed about just a few years ago. But now the Democrats smell blood. They're outraising us, outspending us, and targeting our Senate seats -- even in red states

**[They want revenge.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zqvuuo1q8r5qs27b9ha3dd2imnfufq37gobgrtbg_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)**

If we lose this next election, everything we just won could vanish. They'll tear it all down.

That's why I'm writing you -- not for $10, not for $100 -- just for $1. Not because $1 will flip the Senate, but because it's a signal that you haven't given up.
[CHIP IN $1 NOW](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zqdfo582toub2qqotcuukib9m5mdh44j2hnvrcr0_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)
Your name on our donor list tells me we're not alone -- that this still matters to you.
Thank you,

[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z84oeirtoe1lqjmbi3a3smr4d5k3h2tctcfecun8_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)
Ted Cruz

[CHIP IN $1 NOW](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z3ke3m4i8rsebvnemb95vjevhhc1iv9ego8478f0_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)

| Paid for by the NRSC.
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
[www.NRSC.org](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zdvuq927qf3td8bdkhg5ks1739sk9uumuaqje380_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)
---

Pop Acta Media | American Action News
---

_[**unsubscribe**](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z91v47b8s8m9vsg3m4v9uj3r01v2o1lapbcq0k70_rp2ash2s8gb2sbgebjqf9tge5rrasbjkgqracjkmoq37d1m65qbfctjmkrncst66uqrf9bjmpqn4djm69qb4d9nkvijbo9getqb3cdkmppmeo0n6mq39cdjkorrbcp4mni62pr0shh6coh1k8gn48n24hjmaob1chim9oj6c8iv0)_ | _[manage data preferences](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zfmhdpn8e2dcps3pbpts02c5kocvfggsne43975o_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph2bs)_

[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zpbcssgh677qf08mb4hinqjmogdec5df3b2eu4rg_rp22sh2s8i6epb1c5i6ap72cph238h5e8he49h666qbcdli6uon49fg)

name: SECOND ATTEMPT -- D.C. Statehood Petition [via Fight for Reform]
subject: maybe we were wrong… maybe you don’t care about this?
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
| Fight for Reform is dedicated to electing Democrats, transforming our broken campaign finance system, and ending _Citizens United_ state by state. If you would like to get fewer emails, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59469?t=1&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x) If you would like to unsubscribe, [**_click here._**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/59470?t=2&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x) Are you getting our emails, friend?

[As one of our most engaged supporters in your state, we assumed you’d want to sign our petition urging Congress to make D.C. a state.](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65499?t=3&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x) **It’s a big deal:** Pete Buttigieg, Jamie Raskin, Jasmine Crockett, Joe Biden, and Elizabeth Warren are fighting for it tooth and nail...

...Because if D.C. became the 51st state, we’d gain two more Democratic Senators – _we’d have a clear path to the Senate Majority!!_ [**Maybe we were wrong. Maybe you don’t care. But if you do, sign the petition before it closes tomorrow morning at 9AM:**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65499?t=4&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x) | [URGE CONGRESS TO MAKE D.C. A STATE >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65499?t=5&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x)
---
Thanks, friend.

Fight for Reform

— FORWARDED MESSAGE —

**Listen up:**
**WE TOLD YOU:** Pete Buttigieg, Jamie Raskin, Jasmine Crockett, Joe Biden, and Elizabeth Warren all want Washington, DC to become the 51st STATE!

**WE TOLD YOU:** If DC became the 51st state, 700,000 Americans would get representation in Congress AND we’d gain 2 more Democratic Senators!

**WE TOLD YOU:** Donald Trump and the GOP HATE the idea of DC becoming a state because it would **WRECK their far-right agenda.** **So, we’re rushing to gather 20,000 signatures before 11:59 PM so that we can reach our goal and PRESSURE CONGRESS TO MAKE DC THE 51ST STATE!**
---

[**we’ve only got 8 hours to reach our midnight goal. Can you sign on now to stand with Top Democrats??**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65499?t=6&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x) [](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65499?t=7&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x)
[ADD MY NAME >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65499?t=8&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x)
---
Listen – We’re counting on Top Democrats like you to step up and help support our campaign for DC Statehood.

DC has a larger population than Vermont and Wyoming but they have absolutely 0 voting representation in Congress... It's disgusting!

**Trump and his GOP are opposed to DC Statehood because they’re afraid of Democracy and giving power back to the people!**

But, we can’t stand by and idly watch for another second, that’s why we’re penning a massive petition.
[**20,000 SIGNATURES NEEDED BY MIDNIGHT: Add your name to our MASSIVE petition to demand Congress make DC the 51st state! >>**](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65499?t=9&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x) [ADD MY NAME >>](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/65499?t=10&akid=23262%2E4449941%2EmT_y2x)
---
Let’s get this done,

_Fight for Reform _ | This message was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com

name: Niki Conforti
subject: See What Casten Really Supports
body:
See What Casten Really Supports | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/441d01619948/conforti-for-congress-10610198?e=25f4fa3311)
---
| | |
---
| | | Dear Peter,

The recent passage of the Bill Beautiful Bill kept our taxes low. It ensured the earlier tax cuts were made permanent and instituted additional tax breaks for seniors and the middle class.

Instead of helping Americans, Sean Casten fell in-line once again, standing with his party to vote against passage of the BBB. By doing so, he has voted against the people of this district - and all Americans. In essence, he voted in favor of the following:
1. He voted to give illegals Medicaid \- at your expense
2. He voted for a historic tax increase on the middle class
3. He voted to tax your tips and overtime
4. He voted to slash the child tax credit
5. He voted for open borders and to keep criminal illegals in our country
6. He voted for increased regulations on business and families

He does not represent your best interests in Congress and is working against us. It's time to _Vote Him Out_!!

When I have the honor of representing the people of the 6th District in Congress, I will work to better your lives by promoting family values, safe communities, and a growing economy.

Help us spread the word for the Conforti for Congress campaign!

Please consider a contribution to help us flip District 6 and Cast out Casten! Your support will go a long way in helping us retake the district - and turn it RED.

Click the link below to make a donation today.

[](https://conforti4congress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7fd34b7284bf7f5e87a4e45e5&id=43fcc2d09d&e=25f4fa3311)

Don't stop believing in what we can achieve when we work together!
Your support is greatly appreciated.
---
| Thank you,

Niki Conforti
Candidate
Illinois 6th Congressional District
---
| PS: Follow my campaign on [Facebook](https://conforti4congress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7fd34b7284bf7f5e87a4e45e5&id=58eb244b74&e=25f4fa3311), [Twitter](https://conforti4congress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7fd34b7284bf7f5e87a4e45e5&id=c136e53b54&e=25f4fa3311), and [Instagram](https://conforti4congress.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=7fd34b7284bf7f5e87a4e45e5&id=9d46e7eaad&e=25f4fa3311)!

name: Secretary Patrick J. Murphy
subject: Lessons Learned from Being Unemployed
body:
The Hardest Job I Ever Had Was Finding One After the Military

| |
---|---|---
| | | Forwarded this email? [Subscribe here](https://substack..lC0d_3pHi-533CPm-VlyTOpORLwr4wwoIq95r9t1VuY?) for more
---
[](https://substack..wwCmRLN_xPI5yMDmZ_kTmZuBI0DQ_TrYARuiISOyvRI?)
---
# [Lessons Learned from Being Unemployed](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=170170154&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwMTcwMTU0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTQ0MDMzNzQsImV4cCI6MTc1Njk5NTM3NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sEUeufCfb2jhlgueNp1A-E-Mp-SX8MLjEFFRAKU7CsQ)
### The Hardest Job I Ever Had Was Finding One After the Military
| | [Patrick J. Murphy](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---
| Aug 5
---
| [](https://substack.com/@patrickmurphypa)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=170170154&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwMTcwMTU0LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1NDQwMzM3NCwiZXhwIjoxNzU2OTk1Mzc0LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDM5OTEzMyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.QJow8MEvBQBhGbLl_oCvMkgdwo08RRunZuKp5r3B1QA&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=170170154&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwMTcwMTU0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTQ0MDMzNzQsImV4cCI6MTc1Njk5NTM3NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sEUeufCfb2jhlgueNp1A-E-Mp-SX8MLjEFFRAKU7CsQ&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4399133&post_id=170170154&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTcwMTcwMTU0LCJpYXQiOjE3NTQ0MDMzNzQsImV4cCI6MTc1Njk5NTM3NCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQzOTkxMzMiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sEUeufCfb2jhlgueNp1A-E-Mp-SX8MLjEFFRAKU7CsQ)
---
| | [](https://substack..sNGnuzRPJFt_RTeSfJ5byf0sQ4Q11A4uF3tTSciNAUQ?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/patrickmurphypa/p/lessons-learned-from-being-unemployed?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I've never told this story--not to my friends, not to my ex-wife, my kids, not to the troops I've worked with, and certainly not publicly to the 240,000+ of you reading this via Substack. (Thank you, by the way.)
But it's time.
Because when I came home from Iraq and left Fort Bragg, I was unemployed. For months.
If you had seen me then, you'd have no idea how badly I was struggling. On the surface, everything looked okay. I seemed to have it all together. I kept up appearances, wearing slacks, a dress shirt, and a blazer in public. I looked professional enough to disguise the fact that I had just $322 left in my bank account. "Never let them see you sweat," right? But inside, I was sweating buckets.
On paper, my military career was off the charts successful. I had just returned from two overseas deployments. I had prosecuted terrorists in Baghdad, taught cadets as the youngest professor at West Point, and served as a Special Assistant U.S. Attorney. But when I came home to Philadelphia in 2004, it didn't translate to a paycheck.
The Post-9/11 GI Bill didn't exist yet. There was no TAP program (Transition Assistance Program). VA Home Loans weren't what they are today. Transitioning from the military back then felt like walking a tightrope without a safety net.
[Support Stories That Matter](https://substack.com/redirect/0198ff47-24af-452c-bc8d-bed4437a0d9b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
I was too proud (or maybe just too embarrassed) to apply for public assistance. I was a blue-collar guy with a law degree, the first in my family to graduate college. My parents were still working in public service two hours away, and after everything--the deployments and combat--it didn't feel right to ask Uncle Sam for unemployment benefits, even though I had paid into them. I found myself back to donating plasma every week for $25 just to get by.
Even as I was struggling, I was blessed in so many ways. For one, I had survived combat duty in Iraq, which took 19 of my colleagues' lives; I was one of the lucky ones. But I was facing new battles back home.
To snap out of my funk, I rented a room for $500 month in Philly and volunteered full-time on the Kerry campaign for two months. Then found myself unemployed for four months. I was grateful to have enough to live off my deployment savings, but I was living very frugally. My diet was a strict rotation of ramen noodles and peanut butter & jelly.
It was very isolating dealing with this on my own. And yet, I wasn't alone. Roughly **[53% of Post‐9/11 veterans experienced a period of unemployment](https://substack.com/redirect/58b2015c-a3ff-4a46-864c-cd527ced58b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** immediately following discharge. Back then, we didn't know we were living through a crisis, part of the civilian-military divide. But we didn't talk about it. There was no LinkedIn. There were just veterans like me quietly grinding through, hoping things would eventually click. Part of it was irrational confidence in myself.
The support networks we have now didn't exist then. When I left Fort Bragg, North Carolina, we didn't have a career counselor or a transition program. The Post-9/11 GI Bill--which I later co-authored--wasn't even an idea yet. We were on our own.
Job interviews were brutal. Imagine sitting across from partners at a law firm explaining how prosecuting a high-level terrorist in Baghdad under Iraq's 1969 criminal code is "relevant" experience. (Not exactly what they teach in law school.) Or how making an argument a couple feet away from a known terrorist inside the Central Criminal Court of Iraq, with paratroopers on alert behind me, was just as intense as federal court and more than prepared me for the pressures of corporate litigation.
The law firms liked the stories. They just didn't hire me. I knew I wasn't going to get an offer when I heard: "Thank you for your service."
I thought surviving Iraq would be the hard part. But in many ways, coming home was even harder. Navigating the civilian job market as part of the first wave of veterans returning wasn't easy.
I had to constantly pitch my career, and most of the time, the answer was a polite "Thank you, but..." I remember thinking, "I survived combat, but this rejection is its own kind of battle."
I was gutting through it. But I promised myself, if I ever made it through, I'd make damn sure the next wave of veterans wouldn't have to do it alone.
From the Desk of Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..yD0ZEO65l0LHMzE06oHZHjRluZhgIsMsf1UhjTC2j08?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=170170154)
## **From "No Job" to Coauthoring the Post-9/11 GI Bill**
Fast forward to 2007. I'm a newly elected member of Congress. My roommate at the time was Tim Walz--yes,_that_ Tim Walz, current Governor of Minnesota and Kamala Harris's former running mate. We were both veterans who had dealt with our own messy transitions.
We met during congressional orientation and bonded instantly over two things: our military service and our shared status as two of the least wealthy members of Congress. So naturally, we became roommates, splitting rent on the cheapest place we could find.
Together, we rolled up our sleeves with Senator Jim Webb and got to work on the **Post-9/11 GI Bill** , determined to build a bridge of opportunity for our veteran brothers and sisters that didn't exist when we came home. Tim Walz was an instant leader in the Veterans Affairs Committee as I served on the Armed Services.
Today, over 800,000 veterans are using the Post-9/11 GI Bill. They're graduating at higher rates than their civilian peers, earning higher GPAs, and are more likely to pursue STEM degrees. That's one of my proudest congressional achievements.
Nowadays, I'm blessed to be part of the [Hiring Our Heroes](https://substack.com/redirect/0e730f51-9ef4-41d8-8ce4-ea049d67f4fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) team at the U.S. Chamber Foundation. We help over 80,000 transitioning veterans and military spouses every year. Just last week, I stood tall in front of 450 troops, giving them a talk before they interviewed with over 60 employers at Joint Base McGuire-Dix-Lakehurst. I gave them every piece of advice I could. How to network. How to navigate interviews. How to stay resilient. How to be ready to accept a 'No' \- but to find the confidence & resiliency to drive on with grit.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/45973959-9360-4999-897c-c67abfb05dca?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Me speaking at the Hiring Our Heroes Career Summit last week.
I them every piece of advice I had--except for the most personal one.
I didn't tell them that I, too, had been broke, unemployed, and floundering after taking off the uniform. That I knew exactly how it felt to sit at the kitchen table, looking at a dwindling bank balance, paying student loans, and calculating how many PB&J sandwiches you can make with what's left. I'm embarrassed to talk about it today. It's easier to write about it than to talk about it.
Today, because of the efforts of Tim Walz, Jim Webb, and so many other great Americans, [veteran unemployment sits around 3%](https://substack.com/redirect/4a36c281-3a39-43df-b442-e391e5f39072?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) which is below the[ national average (~4 %)](https://substack.com/redirect/e4e4b264-86c7-4b90-87b8-71b560390c8d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). However, **younger veterans continue to face real challenges**. In May 2025, [veterans aged ](https://substack.com/redirect/2c852344-9e63-45d8-9148-4805ecea1fbe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[18-24 had an unemployment rate of 9.3%](https://substack.com/redirect/2c852344-9e63-45d8-9148-4805ecea1fbe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**, slightly higher than their non-veteran peers. Military spouses face even steeper barriers. While the overall adult unemployment rate is about 4%, **military spouses report an unemployment rate of around[21%](https://substack.com/redirect/7106c456-ba21-4599-8637-bbae4c2550b0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).**
I talk a lot about moral courage. About how our leaders often lack it. But last week, as I stood there encouraging these troops to be open about their struggles, it hit me in the gut: I wasn't practicing what I preached.
So here's me rectifying that.
## **3 Lessons from My Unemployment**
Whether you're a veteran or just someone in between jobs (or lost in general), these lessons apply to you:
### **1\. Transitions Are Hard. You 're Not In This Alone.**
Every year, 200,000 servicemembers & military spouses transition out. It's a churn that doesn't stop. Hiring Our Heroes hosts over 80 events a year to help them. My advice: Don't fall through the cracks. Take ownership of your journey. Don't necessarily jump at the first job just because it's there or the highest paying. Be strategic about what you really want. The right job in the right industry, at the right time, can change your life. Have the patience to wait for it. And actively seek it out.
### **2\. Your Network Is Your Net Worth.**
Take out your phone. Right now. Open LinkedIn. Connect with me. I mean it--I'll accept your invite (even if you don't write a note). And if I can't help directly, I'll introduce you to someone who can. Relationships matter. A Zoom call is better than a phone call. An in-person meeting is better than a Zoom. Make the effort. Be social. Cultivate relationships. People are willing to help if you ask. But ask for advice, not a job. If you ask for a job, you'll get advice. If you ask for advice, you're more likely to get a job.
### **3\. Plan Your Work and Work Your Plan.**
Set weekly goals. Maybe it's five new connections a week. Prepare your Ethos (your credibility), your Logos (your data, your argument), and your Pathos (describe your passion). That's Aristotle's formula, and it still works over 2,000 years later. It's what I teach my students at Wharton. You have to make people care. You have to make them believe. And you have to keep going after hearing "No" a hundred times.
People tell me "No" every day. (in business, dating, in life generally, and that's ok). That's life. What matters is finding the "Yes" that aligns with your purpose.
They say a smart person learns from their own mistakes, but a wise person learns from someone else's. So be wise. Learn from me.
Don't be too proud to ask for help. Be raw. Be authentic. Pay it forward. Be prepared. And believe that tomorrow will be better--because it's in your power to make it so.
From the Desk of Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

-- Patrick
_The Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a Wharton lecturer, Vetrepreneur, and the 32nd Army Under Secretary after earning the Bronze Star for service in Baghdad, Iraq as an All-American with the 82nd Airborne Division --@PatrickMurphyPA on Instagram and Twitter._

You 're Not In This Alone. Every year, 200,000 servicemembers & military spouses transition out. It's a churn that doesn't stop. Hiring Our Heroes hosts over 80 events a year to help them. My advice: Don't fall through the cracks. Take ownership of your journey. Don't necessarily jump at the first job just because it's there or the highest paying. Be strategic about what you really want. The right job in the right industry, at the right time, can change your life. Have the patience to wait for it. And actively seek it out. 2\. Your Network Is Your Net Worth. Take out your phone. Right now. Open LinkedIn. Connect with me. I mean it--I'll accept your invite (even if you don't write a note). And if I can't help directly, I'll introduce you to someone who can. Relationships matter. A Zoom call is better than a phone call. An in-person meeting is better than a Zoom. Make the effort. Be social. Cultivate relationships. People are willing to help if you ask. But ask for advice, not a job. If you ask for a job, you'll get advice. If you ask for advice, you're more likely to get a job. 3\. Plan Your Work and Work Your Plan. Set weekly goals. Maybe it's five new connections a week. Prepare your Ethos (your credibility), your Logos (your data, your argument), and your Pathos (describe your passion). That's Aristotle's formula, and it still works over 2,000 years later. It's what I teach my students at Wharton. You have to make people care. You have to make them believe. And you have to keep going after hearing "No" a hundred times. People tell me "No" every day. (in business, dating, in life generally, and that's ok). That's life. What matters is finding the "Yes" that aligns with your purpose. They say a smart person learns from their own mistakes, but a wise person learns from someone else's. So be wise. Learn from me. Don't be too proud to ask for help. Be raw. Be authentic. Pay it forward. Be prepared. And believe that tomorrow will be better--because it's in your power to make it so. From the Desk of Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. -- Patrick The Honorable Patrick J. Murphy is a Wharton lecturer, Vetrepreneur, and the 32nd Army Under Secretary after earning the Bronze Star for service in Baghdad, Iraq as an All-American with the 82nd Airborne Division --@PatrickMurphyPA on Instagram and Twitter. You're currently a free subscriber to . For the full experience, | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Patrick J. Murphy ? ?

subject: Thank Texas Democrats for fighting back hard against Republican attacks on democracy
body:
| | | | |

| | |
# [Thank Texas Democrats for fighting back hard against Republican attacks on democracy](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc71a6913-8322-4d14-85b7-f953ee304249/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/T2b55c7b2-febe-4df2-94b4-1324262db7fd/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Texas Republicans just launched a full-on attack on democracy, suddenly redrawing districts in absurd ways that aim to make Congressional elections irrelevant, and ensure Republican victories. Without debate, without discussion, and without regard to the law or the Constitution. **But[Texas Democrats fought back hard and smart](https://default.salsalabs.org/T75e18368-e63d-4ad5-913f-fbb0b6d9bdd7/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9). Republicans might have the votes to pass the changes, but they can only hold the vote if they have a quorum (a minimum number of members present). So Texas Democrats went on a road trip. They left the state -- meaning no quorum = no vote.** Republicans have gone ballistic, threatening to arrest the Democrats, put them in jail, and call them mean names. But they couldn't force them to come back. Eventually, the Republicans will likely get their way. But at least temporarily, Democrats have been able to halt the GOP action long enough to shine a spotlight on a thoroughly un-American move Republicans had hoped to carry out quickly and quietly. And they've given Democrats across the nation a lesson in how to do more than just write sternly-worded letters. And that is already giving an added burst of momentum to [Democratic counter-attacks](https://default.salsalabs.org/T54f6275e-eb56-4acc-8571-3afff5b324e2/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9). [**Please join us, add your name, and thank Texas Democrats for fighting back hard against Republican attacks on democracy.**](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tabca0a3d-73d2-46fd-b3b5-4f6de7e9f256/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | | [ADD YOUR NAME >>](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tdd354280-e31f-44c3-8bcf-7fa2ee6a845b/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | Thank you, Left Action
---
|
---

* Follow us on [Facebook](https://default.salsalabs.org/T4950d2ec-ccc1-4154-a15b-0f70e6ece340/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9) and [Twitter](https://default.salsalabs.org/T505d8baa-5230-4232-ab63-a08b27638f3c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
* [Go here for more actions you can take](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tbe93347a-54f4-4d7e-a9c9-19c7fa23bf3c/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
